source: DealOrNoDeal (Lewis et al. 2017, MIT license, via stanfordnlp/cocoa)

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: hey there . i'd like the books and the hats in exchange for the balls .

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: how about i have one of the hats and all the balls .

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: my offer is firm based on the point distribution . going for the best deal for the both of us .

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: i get three points total with just balls . how is that fair ?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: i'll then take the three balls and book in exchange for 1 hat if the hat's are of value to you .

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: i was trying to compromise and give you part of something you seemed to want . seriously , 1 hat and 3 balls gives me 5 points .

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: appears our point distirbution is different . i will concede ; i get 1 book , 1 hat . . you get 3 balls , and 1 hat ?
